summary:
The agent confirms through trusted health authorities like the CDC that rotavirus vaccines exist, are part of standard childhood vaccination programs, and have significantly reduced cases and mortality worldwide due to well-documented efficacy across multiple studies and public health reporting systems.

The agent concludes based on verified medical literature and official recommendations—such as those from the CDC—that rotavirus vaccines are not only available but also highly effective, commonly administered, and associated with major decreases in illness severity and death rates in pediatric populations.
